groups.instruction: Answer the questions based on the audio.
groups.prompt_html:
MULTIPLE CHOICE 10

Questions 21 - 26

For each question, choose THREE correct options.

Write them in any order in answer boxes 21 - 26.

NORTH SEA OIL AND GAS INDUSTRY BACKGROUND

Which THREE of the following are true of the early development of the North Sea oil and gas industry?

21

22

23

AIt had long been suspected that oil might be present under the North Sea.
BThe first major discovery of gas in the North Sea took place in the late 1950s.
CThe British were the first to discover oil and gas in the North Sea.
DThe site of the first accident was a British oil rig.
EThe industry was slow to develop due to an economic crisis at the time.
FThe oil and gas industry was seen as a possible solution to a national problem.

Which THREE of the following describe the North Sea oil and gas industry's subsequent history?

24

25

26

AMany more sites have been found since the original discoveries.
BThe area has been open to use by companies from additional countries.
CThe industry has suffered no setbacks in its 50-year history.
DIts most productive period was around the turn of the millennium.
EIt produces more oil than any other area in the world.
FThe North Sea is expected to continue to produce large quantities of oil.

items.prompt_html: | A | It had long been suspected that oil might be present under the North Sea. |
| A | Many more sites have been found since the original discoveries. |

options.label: A
options.text: Many more sites have been found since the original discoveries.

options.label: B
options.text: The area has been open to use by companies from additional countries.

options.label: C
options.text: The industry has suffered no setbacks in its 50-year history.

options.label: D
options.text: Its most productive period was around the turn of the millennium.

options.label: E
options.text: It produces more oil than any other area in the world.

options.label: F
options.text: The North Sea is expected to continue to produce large quantities of oil.
